side_effects.description: BP elevated to 170/98 at home, 170/110 at ER; treated with clonidine; resolved to 127/80 at discharge
Felt like she was going to pass out

protocol_notes: OP is a 34-year-old female, 5'5", 150 lbs, 6 years sober, history of preeclampsia requiring emergency c-section and magnesium drip. BP was 170/98 at home, 170/110 at ER. Treated with clonidine in ER, discharged at 127/80. Fatigue, headache, and high BP resolved upon stopping the peptide. Had an NAD+ injection approximately one week prior with no adverse effects beyond brief flushing. COVID and flu tests negative at ER. OP discontinued BPC-157 and reported feeling significantly better. ER doctor attributed symptoms to peptide and advised stopping for a few days before retrying. Prescribing doctor also suspected BPC-157. Dose was 0.5mg morning and night subcutaneously (implied by context).
